sanskrit: किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं चतेजोराशिं सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम् । पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥
hindiTranslation: मैं आपको किरीट, गदा, चक्र तथा शङ्ख और पद्म धारण किये हुए देख रहा हूँ ।  आपको तेजकी राशि, सब ओर प्रकाश करनेवाले, देदीप्यमान अग्नि तथा सूर्यके समान कान्तिवाले, नेत्रोंके द्वारा कठिनतासे देखे जानेयोग्य और सब तरफसे अप्रमेयस्वरूप देख रहा हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: I see you wearing a crown, holding a mace and discus, blazing everywhere, hard to behold, and immeasurable — like fire and the sun in brilliance.
essence: What is most real cannot be fully held by sight.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna sees Krishna in a radiant cosmic form, crowned and armed, blazing like fire and the sun, impossible to measure or fully look at.
simpleInsight: Awe replaces ordinary seeing. The mind meets something too vast to contain.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still staring at Krishna’s cosmic form after the armies have frozen around them. What he sees is overwhelming: crowned, armed, blazing, and beyond measure. This vision deepens his surrender before Krishna explains what this vast form means.
modernRelevance: You stand before a decision that strips away all comfort: the relationship, the job, the truth you have avoided. Suddenly the situation feels larger than your plans, and your control looks small.
translationLiteral: किरीटिनम् (crowned) / गदिनम् (mace-bearing) / चक्रिणम् (discus-bearing) / च (and) / तेजोराशिम् (a mass of radiance) / सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम् (shining on all sides) / पश्यामि (I see) / त्वाम् (you) / दुर्निरीक्ष्यम् (hard to behold) / समन्तात् (on every side) / दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिम् (with the brilliance of blazing fire and the sun) / अप्रमेयम् (immeasurable)
insight: It shifts the mind from trying to grasp the whole picture to yielding before what exceeds comprehension.
